Generated by Spark: Mirror-Mirror: Application Design SpecificationsVersion: 2.1 (Resolved)Date: December 30, 2025Document Type: Product Design Specification (PDS)1. Executive SummaryMirror-Mirror is a pioneering "Phygital" (Physical + Digital) ecosystem designed to bridge the widening gap between hyper-realistic Virtual Try-On (VTO) utility and the logistical realities of Professional Cosmetology Operations.The current market is bifurcated: on one side, consumers use standalone VTO apps that function largely as novelty filters, offering "magical" but physically inaccurate transformations. On the other, salon professionals rely on rigid, calendar-based software that manages bookings but lacks any visual or diagnostic context. Mirror-Mirror integrates these two isolated worlds.It functions as a dual-sided platform. For the consumer, it is a "truth engine"—allowing a user to diagnose skin health and simulate a complex aesthetic change (e.g., "Copper Red Balayage" or "Vintage Dior Dress") using medical-grade AI and physics-based rendering. For the provider, it acts as a qualified lead generator and operational command center, seamlessly booking a professional capable of executing that exact service. All technical data—from hair porosity to skin moisture levels—is transferred securely to the provider’s operational stack, effectively digitizing the consultation before the client ever enters the salon.2. Strategic Pillars & Core PhilosophyBased on extensive industry research, the application is built upon three non-negotiable strategic pillars that define its value proposition:2.1 The Truthful Digital Twin (VTO Tech)We are moving the industry standard away from mesh-based rendering—which often results in a "cartoonish" or "video game" aesthetic—toward 3D Gaussian Splatting. This volumetric technique allows for the real-time, photorealistic visualization of complex textures like frizzy hair, sheer fabric, and translucent skin. By simulating how light scatters through volume rather than just bouncing off a surface, we ensure the "Uncanny Valley" is bridged. The goal is not to make the user look better, but to make the digital representation look real, building the trust necessary for high-stakes purchases.2.2 Privacy-First Architecture (Legal/BIPA)In response to the litigious landscape surrounding biometric data (specifically the Illinois Biometric Information Privacy Act), Mirror-Mirror adopts a strict Zero-Knowledge Architecture. All biometric processing—facial geometry mapping, iris tracking, and skin analysis—occurs exclusively on the user's local device (Edge Computing). We treat face data as toxic waste: we do not want to hold it. By ensuring user face geometry never touches the cloud, we eliminate massive liability risks and offer users a "safe harbor" for their digital identity.2.3 Invisible Operations (Salon Tech)We utilize Precision Scheduling with algorithmic gap processing logic to solve the "Swiss Cheese" calendar problem—fragmented schedules with unusable downtime. Combined with "invisible" payments, we aim to remove every point of friction from the booking and transaction experience. The technology should recede into the background, allowing the stylist to focus entirely on the craft and the client connection.3. Business Model & MonetizationTo align the interests of consumers, stylists, and the platform, Mirror-Mirror employs a robust B2B2C Hybrid Model:Freemium Consumer App (The Hook): The core VTO engine, Trend Carousel, and basic "Playground" editing tools are free. This drives mass adoption and trains the AI models."Mirror+" Subscription (The Upsell): For $9.99/mo, power users gain access to 'Medical-Grade Analysis' (tracking skin health over time), unlimited high-fidelity renders, and exclusive 'Designer Drops' in the virtual wardrobe.SaaS for Salons (The Recurring Revenue): Professionals and salons pay a tiered subscription (e.g., $40 - $165/mo) for the "Booking Bridge" and proprietary 'Gap Scheduling' logic. This replaces their existing, less intelligent booking software.Transaction Fee (The Yield): A standard 2.6% + $0.10 processing fee on all 'Invisible Checkout' payments, competitive with Square and Stripe but integrated deeply into the specific workflow of the salon.4. User PersonasPrimary Persona: The "Smart" Client (Alice)Profile: 28-year-old marketing manager, heavy social media user.Goal: Wants to know definitively if a specific haircut or skincare regimen will work for her specific face shape before committing time and money.Pain Point: She suffers from "Bracketing" (ordering three sizes of the same dress to return two) and "Salon Regret" (agreeing to a cut she hates immediately). She is tired of filters that make her look like an alien.Tech Usage: Owns an iPhone 15 Pro+ (LiDAR enabled), uses Apple Pay for everything, and values data privacy.Secondary Persona: The Data-Driven Stylist (Leo)Profile: 35-year-old senior colorist and salon owner.Goal: Wants to reduce unpaid consultation time (currently 15 mins per client) and increase retail upsell conversion rates.Pain Point: Clients bring unrealistic Pinterest photos of edited models that do not match their actual hair texture or skin tone. He also struggles with "Swiss Cheese" schedules where 30-minute gaps go unfilled.Tech Usage: Uses an iPad Pro at his station but hates switching between three different apps for booking, payments, and inventory.5. Feature SpecificationsModule A: "The Reflection" (Consumer VTO Engine)This module represents the consumer-facing interface, combining entertainment with utility.A.1 The 3D Gaussian Splatting EngineFunction: The core rendering pipeline that visualizes apparel and hair in real-time. Unlike traditional polygons, which struggle with "fuzzy" edges (like flyaway hairs or lace), Gaussian Splats represent the subject as a cloud of 3D ellipses.Tech Stack: Custom WebGL viewer with .splat file support, optimized for Apple's Metal API and Android's Vulkan.User Flow:User initiates a scan using the front-facing TrueDepth camera (face) or rear LiDAR (body).App generates a sparse point cloud locally.A "Style Request" (e.g., "Vintage Silk Dress") is sent to the cloud.The cloud processes the complex physics of the garment (drape, wind resistance, transparency).The local device receives the physics data and renders the 'Splat' model composite. On devices with A17 Pro chips or equivalent, target frame rates exceed 60 FPS, ensuring fluid motion, while older devices utilize dynamic Level of Detail (LOD) scaling.A.2 The Trend Carousel (Look Library)Function: A dynamic discovery engine that allows rapid cycling through pre-set, trending styles.Mechanism: A "Tinder-style" swipe interface or a bottom-scrolling carousel. Users can instantly apply complex looks like "The Wolf Cut," "90s Blowout," or "Latte Makeup" to their digital twin.Data Source: Trends are aggregated weekly via APIs from social media platforms (TikTok/Instagram), analyzed for engagement velocity, and then curated by a board of partner stylists to ensure quality.Smart Filtering: The library uses the user's biometric data to automatically filter out styles that are physically impossible or highly inadvisable. For example, it will hide "Platinum Blonde" options for a user with Level 1 Black Hair unless the "Extensions/Bleach Damage" toggle is explicitly enabled.A.3 The "Playground" Mode (Interactive Editing)Function: A haptic, gesture-based editing suite that turns the VTO experience into a creative game. It allows users to manually manipulate their look beyond static presets.Tools:Virtual Scissors: Users perform a "scissor pinch" gesture (two fingers closing) or a swipe motion to trim hair length in real-time. The Gaussian Splat engine dynamically updates the alpha (opacity) channels of the splats to simulate hair falling away.Smudge & Blend: A touch-based tool allowing users to use their finger to smudge eyeliner, blend blush, or "mess up" a pristine hairdo to see how it looks "lived-in" or after a night out.Opacity Layering: Users can manually layer different lip shades to create custom colors (e.g., mixing a red gloss over a nude liner), mimicking real-world makeup application.Reset Logic: A "Shake to Undo" gesture combined with a persistent "History Slider" at the bottom of the screen allows users to scrub back through their edits frame-by-frame or instantly revert to the original state.A.4 Medical-Grade Skin & Hair AnalysisFunction: A diagnostic tool that quantifies skin concerns (wrinkles, oiliness, acne, pore size) and hair texture health.Data Source: The AI model is trained on a dataset of 70,000+ medical-grade dermatological images (referencing Perfect Corp findings) to ensure clinical accuracy.Output: The user receives a comprehensive "Mirror Score" (e.g., "Hydration Level: Low - 32%", "Wrinkle Depth: Moderate").Integration: The analysis leads directly to commerce. It automatically recommends specific services (e.g., "HydraFacial" for hydration, "Microneedling" for texture) rather than just retail products, driving higher-value bookings.A.5 The Agentic AI StylistFunction: An LLM-based personal assistant that contextualizes the VTO experience using natural language.Capability: It reasons about external factors. Example: "Alice, I see you have a wedding in London next Tuesday. The forecast is rainy. Here is how this trench coat looks on your avatar, and I’ve simulated the 'frizz effect' of 80% humidity on your hair so you can choose the right setting spray."Input: User's Calendar (Location/Event type), Weather API, and the user's Digital Wardrobe database.Module B: "The Connection" (Professional Booking Bridge)This module interfaces with the salon's operations, converting VTO dreams into booked reality.B.1 Look-to-Book WorkflowConcept: The VTO simulation is the booking inquiry. We eliminate the "Can you do this?" phone call.Flow:Alice simulates "Platinum Blonde Bob" in Module A.She clicks "Make it Real."Mirror-Mirror filters local providers based on Skill Certification (verified colorists) and Resource Availability (who has a chair open?).The stylist receives a "Digital Consult Card"—a JSON package containing the VTO image, the specific color codes used in the simulation, and Alice's current hair data—before accepting the appointment.B.2 Smart "Gap" SchedulingLogic: The app recognizes the complex, multi-stage nature of salon services.Example: If Alice books a "Platinum Blonde" service (3 hours total), the system automatically identifies the 45-minute "Processing Time" (while the foil is on) in the middle of the appointment.Action: The system visualizes this 45-minute gap as a "Ghost Block" on the stylist's calendar. This block is visible only to the system and potential bookers. The algorithm actively promotes 'Gap-Filler' services to other clients. If the Ghost Block remains unfilled 24 hours prior to the appointment, the system automatically 'Releases' the block, notifying the stylist of a break or allowing them to manually squeeze in a walk-in.B.3 The Visual Intake FormFunction: Replaces the archaic paper clipboard and PDF forms.Feature: Users sign BIPA consent forms digitally via the app. Crucially, the "Skin Analysis" data from Module A automatically pre-fills the "Concerns" and "Medical History" sections of the intake form.Output: Standardized SOAP Notes (Subjective, Objective, Assessment, Plan) are auto-generated for the esthetician based on the AI scan, saving 5-10 minutes of manual charting time per client.Module C: "The Exchange" (Financial Infrastructure)This module handles the "Invisible" operational layer.C.1 Invisible CheckoutConcept: An "Uber-like" payment experience that removes the transactional friction at the end of a service.Mechanism: A validated Card-on-file is captured at the moment of booking (Module B).Flow: Appointment ends $\rightarrow$ Client walks out the door $\rightarrow$ Geo-fencing triggers (or Stylist performs a "one-tap" completion) $\rightarrow$ Payment is processed in the background.Tipping Logic: Tips are calculated automatically based on the user's pre-set preference (e.g., "Always tip 20%" or "Prompt me later"), avoiding the awkward "turning the iPad around" moment.Split Logic: If Alice bought Retail (Product A) and Service (Haircut), the system splits the commission automatically (e.g., 10% to the stylist for retail, 50% for service) based on the salon's pre-configured compensation rules.C.2 Automated "Win-Back" MarketingTrigger: The system detects a lapse in user behavior—Alice hasn't scanned her face in 6 weeks or booked an appointment in 8 weeks.Action: The system generates a personalized "Digital Drop" notification. Instead of generic text ("We miss you!"), it sends a rendered visual: "See how the new Summer Lip Shades look on you, Alice." This lures her back into the VTO ecosystem, reigniting the booking funnel.6. Technical Architecture & Compliance6.1 The "Edge-First" Privacy LayerThis architecture is the critical defense against Illinois BIPA liability and GDPR violations.Biometric Processing: All extraction of facial geometry, nodal points, and texture mapping occurs exclusively on the user's local device (utilizing the iPhone/Android Neural Engine).Data Transmission: Only the results (analysis data) and a Flattened 2D Render of the requested look are transmitted. The raw 3D biometric mesh and point cloud data are never transmitted. The 2D render is shared via an ephemeral link that expires 24 hours after the appointment.Storage: The user's "Digital Twin" is stored in a secure, encrypted enclave on their device, accessible only via local biometric authentication (FaceID/TouchID).6.2 Integration APIMirror-Mirror is designed to coexist with, not replace, the massive salon ERP market.Sync capability: We maintain robust API integrations with market leaders like Vagaro, Mangomint, and Zenoti.Data Pushed: Appointment Requests, Client Profile Details, Retail Purchase History.Data Pulled: Real-time Calendar Availability, Resource Status (e.g., Is Room 1 booked?), and Inventory Levels.7. UI/UX Design Guidelines7.1 The "Truth" ToggleDesign Element: A prominent slider on the main VTO interface.Left Position (Reality): Renders the Unfiltered LiDAR Mesh. This view uses calibrated lighting to highlight skin texture irregularities, asymmetry, and realistic fabric drape (including tight fits). It is the "Diagnostic View" used for accurate sizing and skin analysis.Right Position (Dream): Applies "Idealized" filters—skin smoothing, "golden hour" lighting, and flattering lens distortion. This is the "Social View" optimized for sharing on Instagram/TikTok.Reasoning: This dual-mode approach addresses the "Uncanny Valley" and trust paradox. Users need "Reality" for utility (Will this actually fit?) but crave "Dream" for ego protection and social validation.7.2 The Consultation Dashboard (Stylist View)Layout: A landscape-oriented split-screen interface designed for iPad.Left Panel: A live video feed of the client sitting in the chair.Right Panel: The "Digital Consult Card" showing the client's desired VTO look, overlaid with their technical stats (e.g., "Current hair porosity: High", "Previous Chemical Service: 6 weeks ago").Tools: "Image Markup" support allows the stylist to use an Apple Pencil to draw proposed cutting lines or color placement directly onto the digital twin, ensuring both parties agree on the "inch" to be cut.8. Future Roadmap (2026+)8.1 Smart Mirror HardwareWe plan to deploy a physical hardware implementation for salon retail floors. Powered by Zero10-style technology, these full-length smart mirrors will allow clients to virtually try on inventory (e.g., hair extensions, jewelry) while standing in the salon, bridging the gap between the app and the physical retail space.8.2 NFT/Metaverse Wardrobe InteroperabilityWe will enable the export of the "Digital Twin" and purchased assets to Ready Player Me. This will allow users to wear their "Mirror-Mirror" hairstyle or digital dress inside Netflix games, Decentraland, or Roblox, turning the app into a primary hub for digital identity management.
Initial commit

Instead of generic text ("We miss you!"), it sends a rendered visual: "See how the new Summer Lip Shades look on you, Alice." This lures her back into the VTO ecosystem, reigniting the booking funnel.6. Technical Architecture & Compliance6.1 The "Edge-First" Privacy LayerThis architecture is the critical defense against Illinois BIPA liability and GDPR violations.Biometric Processing: All extraction of facial geometry, nodal points, and texture mapping occurs exclusively on the user's local device (utilizing the iPhone/Android Neural Engine).Data Transmission: Only the results (analysis data) and a Flattened 2D Render of the requested look are transmitted. The raw 3D biometric mesh and point cloud data are never transmitted. The 2D render is shared via an ephemeral link that expires 24 hours after the appointment.Storage: The user's "Digital Twin" is stored in a secure, encrypted enclave on their device, accessible only via local biometric authentication (FaceID/TouchID).6.2 Integration APIMirror-Mirror is designed to coexist with, not replace, the massive salon ERP market.Sync capability: We maintain robust API integrations with market leaders like Vagaro, Mangomint, and Zenoti.Data Pushed: Appointment Requests, Client Profile Details, Retail Purchase History.Data Pulled: Real-time Calendar Availability, Resource Status (e.g., Is Room 1 booked?), and Inventory Levels.7. UI/UX Design Guidelines7.1 The "Truth" ToggleDesign Element: A prominent slider on the main VTO interface.Left Position (Reality): Renders the Unfiltered LiDAR Mesh. This view uses calibrated lighting to highlight skin texture irregularities, asymmetry, and realistic fabric drape (including tight fits). It is the "Diagnostic View" used for accurate sizing and skin analysis.Right Position (Dream): Applies "Idealized" filters—skin smoothing, "golden hour" lighting, and flattering lens distortion. This is the "Social View" optimized for sharing on Instagram/TikTok.Reasoning: This dual-mode approach addresses the "Uncanny Valley" and trust paradox. Users need "Reality" for utility (Will this actually fit?) but crave "Dream" for ego protection and social validation.7.2 The Consultation Dashboard (Stylist View)Layout: A landscape-oriented split-screen interface designed for iPad.Left Panel: A live video feed of the client sitting in the chair.Right Panel: The "Digital Consult Card" showing the client's desired VTO look, overlaid with their technical stats (e.g., "Current hair porosity: High", "Previous Chemical Service: 6 weeks ago").Tools: "Image Markup" support allows the stylist to use an Apple Pencil to draw proposed cutting lines or color placement directly onto the digital twin, ensuring both parties agree on the "inch" to be cut.8. Future Roadmap (2026+)8.1 Smart Mirror HardwareWe plan to deploy a physical hardware implementation for salon retail floors. Powered by Zero10-style technology, these full-length smart mirrors will allow clients to virtually try on inventory (e.g., hair extensions, jewelry) while standing in the salon, bridging the gap between the app and the physical retail space.8.2 NFT/Metaverse Wardrobe InteroperabilityWe will enable the export of the "Digital Twin" and purchased assets to Ready Player Me. This will allow users to wear their "Mirror-Mirror" hairstyle or digital dress inside Netflix games, Decentraland, or Roblox, turning the app into a primary hub for digital identity management.
Initial commit

Generated by Spark: Project Spec: The Invisible Ledger (v1.0) 1. Executive Summary 1.1 The Strategic Pivot: Invisible Technology in a Hyper-Digital Age The "Invisible Ledger" (Unique ID: TAX-OPS-2025-ARCH-01) represents a fundamental divergence from the prevailing trajectory of financial technology. In an era dominated by "Super Apps," gamified engagement, and aggressive data aggregation, a significant and growing market segment is being actively alienated. This specification outlines the architecture for a proprietary internal operating system designed not to engage users more, but to engage them less—automating the backend complexity of tax operations while presenting a "Zero UI" surface to the client. The target demographic for this architecture is bifurcated yet unified by a desire for friction-less, high-privacy interaction. On one side are the Legacy Seniors, a demographic often marginalized by the rapid digitization of essential services. These users face significant barriers to adoption, ranging from visual impairment to cognitive load issues associated with complex authentication flows.1 On the other side is the emerging Counter-Digital class—Privacy Absolutists, Digital Minimalists, and high-net-worth individuals who reject cloud-based aggregation due to surveillance concerns.2 For these users, the value proposition is not convenience via an app, but security via isolation. The core philosophy of the Invisible Ledger is "Decoupling backend efficiency from frontend complexity." Traditional tax platforms force the client to perform the labor of data entry via a "self-service" portal. This platform inverts that model. It functions as an "Invisible Tech" layer that ingests analog inputs—physical mail, courier drop-offs, SMS, and voice—and processes them with the speed of a modern SaaS platform.3 By utilizing open-source tools (Python, Tesseract, PostgreSQL) and strict data isolation protocols (Air-Gapping), the system creates a "fortress of solitude" around client data, minimizing overhead while maximizing security.4 1.2 Architectural Mandate The system is architected to support High-Touch client service through Low-Tech client interfaces, supported by a High-Tech backend. The mandate requires the system to handle the full lifecycle of financial compliance—from the ingestion of a crumpled receipt in a shoebox to the filing of an IRS-compliant return—without requiring the client to log into a web portal. This necessitates a robust "Logistics Engine" capable of tracking physical assets with the same fidelity as digital packets 6, and a "Neuro-Bridge" capable of supporting executive function for neurodivergent clients through "Body Doubling" and visual data mapping.7 2. Market Context and User Psychographics 2.1 The Counter-Digital Demographic The rise of the "Counter-Digital" demographic is a direct response to the "Attention Economy." Research indicates that an increasing number of users are becoming "Privacy Absolutists," driven by concerns over digital disinformation, foreign interference, and the extraction of user data for targeted advertising.2 This group is characterized by a refusal to use standard cloud services (Google Drive, Dropbox) for sensitive financial documents. They demand "Cold Storage" for their tax lives—systems that are physically disconnected from the public internet to prevent data weaponization.9 Simultaneously, "Digital Minimalists" are seeking to reduce screen time and digital noise. For these users, a tax app that sends push notifications is a nuisance. They prefer asynchronous, text-based communication or high-fidelity voice interactions. The Invisible Ledger caters to this by employing a "Dumbphone Interface" (Module D), allowing complex workflows to be managed via simple SMS or voice commands.10 2.2 The Legacy Senior Gap "Legacy Seniors" represent a massive, underserved market. As government services digitize, barriers such as multi-factor authentication and complex navigation structures effectively lock these citizens out of essential services.1 The "Shoebox" model—where a client physically mails documents—remains the preferred workflow for this demographic. However, traditional accounting firms struggle to handle this efficiently. The Invisible Ledger operationalizes this analog workflow using modern "Chain of Custody" tracking software, providing seniors with the comfort of physical interaction and the speed of digital processing.3 2.3 The Neurodivergent Professional Neurodivergent individuals, particularly those with ADHD or Autism, often struggle with the "Executive Function" required for tax compliance. The "ADHD Tax"—financial penalties resulting from procrastination—is a tangible cost for this group.11 Standard apps, with their clutter and notifications, often exacerbate the problem. The Invisible Ledger addresses this via the "Neuro-Bridge" (Module C), which offers "Body Doubling" (working alongside a silent partner) and visual "Money Maps" (Sankey diagrams) to bypass executive dysfunction constraints.8 3. System Architecture and Logic 3.1 High-Level Architectural Logic The system is built on a "Request-Ingest-Process-Verify" loop. This loop is designed to be asynchronous and multi-modal, capable of accepting input from a high-end scanner, a secure courier API, or a text message, and normalizing it into a standard processing pipeline. The Workflow Loop: Request: The system generates a "Call to Action" based on the client's preferred channel (e.g., mailing a physical kit or sending an SMS reminder). Ingest: Inputs are received via non-traditional channels. Physical: Scanned Mail and Courier Drop-off.14 Digital: MMS, Signal, Voice. Air-Gap: Physical USB transfer.15 Process: Data is routed based on Client Psychographics. Standard: Cloud processing via OCR (Tesseract). Air-Gapped: Flags for local-only processing on disconnected machines.16 Neuro-Support: Triggers "Body Double" scheduling.1 Verify: Human-in-the-loop verification for handwriting; "Safety Word" protocols for client communication.17 Output: Physical deliverables (bound returns) or secure "dumbphone" notifications.18 3.2 Technology Stack Selection The technology stack prioritizes Data Sovereignty, Open Source Stability, and Rapid Development.
Initial commit
